sourceMovie.title: The Mist
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Mist works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 28 Years Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Mist clicked for you, 28 Years Later (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: It
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Mist clicked for you, It (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Mist clicked for you, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Mist?
faq.answer: Try 28 Years Later, It, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials—each shares DNA with The Mist while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Mist?
faq.answer: The Mist (2007) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Mist worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
